title: armeria maritima subsp. muelleri
intro: Armeria maritima subsp. muelleri, also known as sea thrift, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the thistle family and is native to the coastal regions of Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Armeria maritima subsp. muelleri include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the dried flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant in tincture or oil form to extract its medicinal properties.
